questions.q: Which group in the p-block elements is known as chalcogens?
questions.explanation: Chalcogens are a group of elements in group 16 (former group VI A) of the periodic table, including oxygen, sulfur, and selenium.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a noble gas?
questions.explanation: Noble gases are elements in group 18 (former group 0) of the periodic table, which includes helium, argon, krypton, and others.

questions.q: What is the general electronic configuration of p-block elements?
questions.explanation: The general electronic configuration of p-block elements is (n-1)d^(1-10) ns^(1-2), where n represents the outermost energy level.

questions.q: Which p-block elements are known as halogens?
questions.explanation: Halogens are a group of elements in group 17 (former group VII A) of the periodic table, including fluorine, chlorine, and iodine.

questions.q: What is the primary characteristic of noble gases?
questions.explanation: Noble gases have low ionization energies due to their stable electronic configurations.

questions.q: Which of the following p-block elements is a metalloid?
questions.explanation: Arsenic is a metalloid, exhibiting properties of both metals and nonmetals.

questions.q: What is the general trend in atomic radii of p-block elements down a group?
questions.explanation: Atomic radii of p-block elements generally decrease down a group due to the increase in effective nuclear charge.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a characteristic of halogens?
questions.explanation: Halogens are highly electronegative, but not highly electropositive.

questions.q: What is the primary use of halogens?
questions.explanation: Halogens are used in the production of disinfectants and pharmaceuticals due to their antimicrobial properties.

questions.q: Which p-block element has the largest atomic radius?
questions.explanation: Oxygen has the largest atomic radius among the given options due to its low effective nuclear charge.

questions.q: What is the primary reason for the low reactivity of noble gases?
questions.explanation: Noble gases have high ionization energies due to their stable electronic configurations, making them unreactive.

questions.q: Which of the following p-block elements is a semiconductor?
questions.explanation: Selenium is a semiconductor, exhibiting properties of both metals and insulators.

questions.q: What is the general trend in electronegativity of p-block elements down a group?
questions.explanation: Electronegativity of p-block elements generally decreases down a group due to the increase in atomic size.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a characteristic of metalloids?
opts: (D) Ability to exhibit both metal and nonmetal properties
questions.explanation: Metalloids do not typically exhibit low ionization energies.

questions.q: What is the primary use of metalloids?
questions.explanation: Metalloids are used in the production of semiconductors due to their ability to exhibit both metal and nonmetal properties.

questions.q: Which p-block element has the highest ionization energy?
questions.explanation: Oxygen has the highest ionization energy among the given options due to its low atomic size and high effective nuclear charge.

questions.q: What is the general trend in the number of valence electrons of p-block elements down a group?
questions.explanation: The number of valence electrons of p-block elements generally decreases down a group due to the increase in atomic size.

questions.q: Which of the following p-block elements is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Selenium is a good conductor of electricity due to its ability to exhibit both metal and nonmetal properties.

questions.q: What is the primary reason for the high reactivity of halogens?
questions.explanation: Halogens are highly electronegative, making them highly reactive.
